channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities
item.description:
Georgia lawmakers have drafted a pair of bills that are set to transform the way math is taught in the state. If the bills become law, they’ll open doors for students to receive a high-quality math education and ultimately shape the direction of their lives.

The post Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story
item.description:
RGF has been very active in opposing the plan to enact a massive gross receipts tax hike at Albuquerque City Council. You can find out more about the plan here and even use the links in the post to reach out to your council members. RGF president Paul Gessing recently sat down with KOAT 7 […]

The post RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJ ED REPORT: As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind?
item.description:
Wells Winegar is Executive Director of the New Jersey Policy Institute, As Governor Mikie Sherrill begins her term in office, one of […]

The post NJ ED REPORT: As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind? first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones
item.description:
Check out NJPI Chairman Andy Mulvihill as he is interviewed by Laura Jones on the landscape of K-12 education in […]

The post INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Senators Brandt and Hallstrom on Property Tax Reform this Year
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Senator Tom Brandt of District 32 and Senator Bob Hallstrom of District 1. In this episode, the senators join Platte Institute CEO Jim Vokal to discuss their efforts to address Nebraska’s property tax burden through LB 1219 and LB 575 (now amended into LB...

The post Nebraskanomics: Senators Brandt and Hallstrom on Property Tax Reform this Year appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] New Jersey Policy Institute Announces Dr. Christopher Young to Board of Directors
item.description:
Parsippany, NJ — The New Jersey Policy Institute (NJPI) today announced that Dr. Christopher Young, an economist and Principal at […]

The post New Jersey Policy Institute Announces Dr. Christopher Young to Board of Directors first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill
item.description:
State legislators are paving the way for children to overcome challenges with literacy, empowering them to thrive in their academic development and build flourishing lives for themselves and their families in the future.

The post Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Regional Passenger Rail Wrong Fit for Portland, Metro Study Shows
item.description:
By Naomi Inman Letter by John A. Charles, Jr. This week, Cascade Policy...

The post Regional Passenger Rail Wrong Fit for Portland, Metro Study Shows appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] The White House Got AI Right. Congress Could Still Get It Wrong.
item.description:
The White House’s National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence is the first serious attempt Washington has made to...

The post The White House Got AI Right. Congress Could Still Get It Wrong. appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] IRS domestic migration data shows another net loss for Kansas and Johnson County
item.description: The 2023 domestic migration data released last week by the Internal Revenue Service shows that Kansas lost $361 million in adjusted gross income (AGI) from U.S. residents moving in and out of the state. That is a smaller loss than in 2022, but more than was lost in 2020 and 2021. Johnson County elected officials […]

item.title: [Mspolicy] Press Release: Mississippi Center for Public Policy Hosts Matt Ridley for Energy and Economic Growth Luncheon
item.description:
JACKSON, MS — The Mississippi Center for Public Policy (MCPP) hosted British author and science writer Matt Ridley for a special luncheon on Thursday, March 19, bringing together policymakers, business leaders, and community members for a discussion on energy, innovation, and economic growth. Ridley delivered an optimistic, data-driven presentation highlighting the critical role of free […]

The post Press Release: Mississippi Center for Public Policy Hosts Matt Ridley for Energy and Economic Growth Luncheon appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Massachusetts lost 145,000 residents to New Hampshire over the last 20 years
item.description:
Massachusetts lost an estimated 145,000 residents to New Hampshire over the last 20 years, a Josiah Bartlett Center analysis of U.S. Census migration data shows. For scale, that number of people living in one place would be Massachusetts’ fourth-largest city, larger than Cambridge and slightly smaller than Springfield.  The U.S. Census Bureau estimates that 369,368 […]

The post Massachusetts lost 145,000 residents to New Hampshire over the last 20 years appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH ranks first in taxpayer ROI for 11th straight year
item.description:
New Hampshire ranked No. 1 in taxpayer return on investment (ROI) for the 11th year in a row, according to financial services website WalletHub.  “New Hampshire is the state with the best taxpayer return on investment, which is due in large part to the fact that it has no state income tax,” WalletHub’s report concludes. […]

The post NH ranks first in taxpayer ROI for 11th straight year appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama poised to make dramatic change to primary voting. The question is – who foots the bill?
item.description:
As Alabama lawmakers grapple with the future of primary elections, the debate returns to a simple question with potentially costly implications: Should taxpayers foot the bill of a closed primary?

The post Alabama poised to make dramatic change to primary voting. The question is – who foots the bill? appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska State Treasurer Joey Spellerberg. During the episode Treasurer Spellerberg and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss LB 938, which creates First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska, the role of the Treasurer’s Office, and solutions for Nebraska’s high property taxes. Listen now on Apple...

The post Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: A Step Forward on Healthcare Reform in Mississippi
item.description:
After a session filled with difficult setbacks and stalled reforms, this week brought a meaningful — and important — step forward. On March 23, HB 1622 was signed into law by the Governor, marking another shift in how Mississippi approaches healthcare regulation. While it is not full repeal of Certificate of Need (CON) laws, it […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: A Step Forward on Healthcare Reform in Mississippi appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Five Retired New York Educators Collect Over $300k In Pensions
item.description:
As state lawmakers consider enhancing retirement benefits for government workers, fresh data from the Empire Center confirm that existing pensions are generous compared to national norms.  For newly retired....

The post Five Retired New York Educators Collect Over $300k In Pensions appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] From the Heartland Post: New IRG Study Shows Exactly How to Win Wisconsin’s Gubernatorial Race
item.description:
New IRG Study Shows Exactly How to Win Wisconsin’s Gubernatorial Race By: Dan O’Donnell View original piece here: https://heartlandpost.com/new-irg-study-shows-exactly-how-to-win-wisconsins-gubernatorial-race/ As Dan O’Donnell writes, a new study from the Institute for Reforming Government shows how the Wisconsin GOP can convince Trump voters who skipped the last governor’s race to vote in huge numbers in 2026. Wisconsin […]

The post From the Heartland Post: New IRG Study Shows Exactly How to Win Wisconsin’s Gubernatorial Race appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Beacontn] Nashville Home-based Business Owners Cement Victory Over Customer Restrictions
item.description: "This awesome victory will help music made by people, together, in Music City, keeping creativity alive.”

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] The Wisconsin experiment in economic freedom
item.description:
Evidence from border counties indicates increased economic freedom encourages prosperity for Wisconsin residents.

The post The Wisconsin experiment in economic freedom appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Benjamin Yount Discusses New IRG and CFFEA Report
item.description:
This morning, radio Show host Benjamin Yount discussed the report we released yesterday along with Citizens for Free Enterprise Action.      

The post Benjamin Yount Discusses New IRG and CFFEA Report appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Democratic Illusion “NO KINGS” RALLIES are gearing up in Indiana for this weekend. It’s a big deal. Forty events are scheduled. Ten Indiana newspapers have signed on with promotional advertisements. It is a good time to review the state of democracy. It’s in bad shape, obviously. We can’t agree on even whether or not elections should be Read the full article...

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Proposed Speed Camera Pilot Could Open the Door to Automated Surveillance
item.description:
Lawmakers in Augusta are currently considering LD 1457, a bill that would authorize the Maine Turnpike Authority to run a pilot program using automated speed enforcement cameras in highway work zones. Supporters argue that the proposal is narrowly tailored and limited in scope, designed simply to improve safety in dangerous construction areas. But beneath the […]

The post Maine’s Proposed Speed Camera Pilot Could Open the Door to Automated Surveillance appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Liberating the Legal System: Beacon Urges Better Access to Justice
item.description: Tennessee has a choice: maintain outdated rules that restrict opportunity and choice or embrace reforms that respect individual rights, encourage productive achievement, and expand access to justice.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Fraud that Wasn’t: Audit Shows Arizona ESA Program 98% Strong
item.description:
By Kathryn Hickok Recent news reporting in Arizona claimed that more than 20...

The post The Fraud that Wasn’t: Audit Shows Arizona ESA Program 98% Strong appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 12
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 12 In this Conference Weekend Edition of Capitol Chat, we break down key dates and legislative processes for the remaining 9 days of the 2026 Session. We also dive into the latest on teacher pay raises, PBM reform, a controversial veto, and banning lab-grown milk.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 12 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Consent of the Governed
item.description: Consent of the Governed  “The United States Government faces growing difficulty in fulfilling its purpose because at least some of its powers are no longer derived from the consent of the governed.”  This was the topic taken on by my Socratic discussion group in March and it proved a difficult one. We never completely resolved Read the full article...

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Tampa Bay Times: Florida to Silicon Valley: We’ll Take It From Here
item.description:
Pollsters love a contradiction, and our new Florida poll seems to deliver one. Seventy-two percent of Floridians want...

The post Tampa Bay Times: Florida to Silicon Valley: We’ll Take It From Here appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] There’s a simple solution to passing an assessment limit if legislators really want to help taxpayers
item.description: The Kansas Legislature concluded its work of the regular session last week amid considerable controversy over an assessment limit that would protect people from unaffordable property tax increases resulting from appraisal increases of 20%, 30%, or even higher. Earlier in the session, the Senate passed a proposal establishing a 3% annual assessment limit, with a […]

item.title: [Will Law] Evers Vetoes New Education Opportunities for Wisconsin Families
item.description:
WILL issued a statement in response to Governor Evers veto of Assembly Bill 602, legislation that would have had the effect of opting Wisconsin into a new federal tax credit scholarship program for K-12 students.

The post Evers Vetoes New Education Opportunities for Wisconsin Families appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Will Law] Evers Vetoes Bipartisan, Pro-Educator Legislation to Encourage Safe Classrooms
item.description:
WILL issued a statement in response to Governor Evers veto of AB 614 / SB 611, known as the Teacher Bill of Rights. The bill would have strengthened teachers’ authority to manage their classrooms, required parental notification when serious classroom incidents occur, and established clearer accountability so teachers can focus on teaching.

The post Evers Vetoes Bipartisan, Pro-Educator Legislation to Encourage Safe Classrooms appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] What Iowa Should Learn From Austin
item.description:
Voters in Austin, Texas overwhelmingly rejected a property tax increase, signaling frustration with rising costs and prompting the city to pursue an independent efficiency audit to improve accountability and control spending. Efficiency audits can identify waste, streamline operations, and generate meaningful savings—often improving services while reducing costs. Local governments in Iowa should undergo the same examination, reflecting the viewpoint of Governor Reynolds that real property tax relief requires structural reforms, transparency, and a willingness to evaluate how local governments operate.

The post What Iowa Should Learn From Austin appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Supports the Ballot Initiative to Reduce the State Income Tax Rate from 5 to 4 Percent to Spur Jobs and Economic Growth
item.description:
At a legislative hearing today before the Special Committee on Initiative Petitions, Pioneer Institute Executive Director Jim Stergios testified on the urgency of addressing Massachusetts’ affordability crisis, slowing job growth, and continued loss of investment, and why a gradual reduction in the...

The post Pioneer Institute Supports the Ballot Initiative to Reduce the State Income Tax Rate from 5 to 4 Percent to Spur Jobs and Economic Growth  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Why Free-Market Capitalism Best Serves Kansas
item.description: Kansas does not need a new economic experiment. It needs more of what actually works. Free-market capitalism remains the best system ever discovered for human flourishing because it respects people as decision-makers, not as subjects of political management. It is built on private property, voluntary exchange, competition, and the rule of law. Those may sound […]

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama shouldn’t apologize for offering religious liberty to our schoolchildren
item.description:
The school choice movement is no longer just a series of isolated legislative wins; it is a sweeping national mandate demanding parental rights and educational freedom.

The post Alabama shouldn’t apologize for offering religious liberty to our schoolchildren appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Getting to the Bottom of the 340B Drug Discount Boondoggle
item.description:
Some of New York’s largest and most prosperous hospitals are reporting rapidly growing amounts of revenue from pharmacy sales –....

The post Getting to the Bottom of the 340B Drug Discount Boondoggle appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Reacts to the Bothfield Redistricting Case Dismissal
item.description:
WILL praised a decision by a circuit court to dismiss a lawsuit against Wisconsin’s current congressional Maps. WILL filed motions to intervene in two lawsuits seeking to overturn Wisconsin’s Congressional Maps. Seeking again to defend those maps on behalf of a group of Wisconsin voters.

The post WILL Reacts to the Bothfield Redistricting Case Dismissal appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Bringing Federal Funds Into the Light
item.description:
The Iowa House is considering a proposal that would require legislative approval before state agencies pursue or accept major federal grants, ensuring that elected lawmakers, not just administrators, have a say in decisions that can commit taxpayers to long-term obligations. The bill mirrors key components of ITR Foundation’s Fiscal Independence Act by combining legislative oversight with greater transparency, including public disclosure of federal guidance that often shapes state policy behind the scenes. Clarity and certainty would be added to complex federal funding, ensuring Iowa fully understands the costs, conditions, and risks attached to federal dollars, helping the state make more informed decisions and better protect taxpayers.

The post Bringing Federal Funds Into the Light appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Dr. Jennifer Doleac: What Actually Deters Crime?
item.description:
Dr. Jennifer Doleac: What Actually Deters Crime? Public safety is foundational to strong communities, but too often, the policies we rely on to reduce crime are driven more by intuition than evidence. As Mississippi and states across the country look for ways to build safer neighborhoods, it’s worth asking a simple question: what actually works?…

The post Dr. Jennifer Doleac: What Actually Deters Crime? first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] North Carolina Surges with 449K Jobs as Massachusetts Jobs Fall by 18K
item.description:
Analysis compares decade-long changes in tax rates, private sector employment, and revenue growth in two competing state economies

The post North Carolina Surges with 449K Jobs as Massachusetts Jobs Fall by 18K appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Transparency - and common sense - win in Idaho legislature
item.description: Across Idaho, citizens and journalists have faced inconsistent rules about whether they could film or record public meetings. In some cases, they were told to stop. In others, policies varied from one city or school district to the next. The result wasn’t transparency—it was confusion.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] How Alabama leaders are addressing social media and online safety
item.description:
Two lawsuits about the harms of social media ended with landmark rulings against social media giants such as Instagram, Facebook and YouTube.

The post How Alabama leaders are addressing social media and online safety appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Cook County TIFs cost taxpayers over $2 billion
item.description:
By freezing property values in a specific area, TIF districts artificially manipulate who pays the levy, leading to effective property tax hikes.

The post Cook County TIFs cost taxpayers over $2 billion appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] New Hire Brings State Government and Oversight Expertise
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE April 1, 2026 Contact: press@reforminggovernment.org  New Hire Brings State Government and Oversight Expertise  Maryjane Behm will immediately help reform and accountability efforts at IRG Action and IRG  Delafield, Wis. – Momentum at the Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) continues to grow as the organization announced that Maryjane “MJ” Behm has joined the […]

The post New Hire Brings State Government and Oversight Expertise appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Beacontn] ExoticRidge v. Hawkins County: Fighting back against an arbitrary data center ban in East Tennessee
item.description: Executive Summary Technology is evolving like never before. Americans today benefit from rapid advancements in fields like artificial intelligence. These cloud-based advancements have a physical component: data centers. The data center industry has already generated trillions of dollars for the U.S. economy. And with companies projecting trillions of dollars more in investments to keep up...

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Middletown Parent Launches Second Petition to Remove State Senator from Black and Puerto Rican Caucus
item.description:
A Middletown mother of three has launched a new petition demanding Sen. Matt Lesser (D-Middletown) be removed from a minority legislative caucus — reigniting a two-year-old controversy over the last-minute […]

The post Middletown Parent Launches Second Petition to Remove State Senator from Black and Puerto Rican Caucus  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as host Jim Vokal rants about rising property taxes, the shell game elected officials are playing, and solutions Platte is advocating for this year. Listen now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

The post Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Politics From the Pews POLITICS IS SOMETHING we don’t talk about in my church — “verboten” my Lutheran grandmother would say. That has never made sense to me. I understand why a pastor may not want to politicize from the pulpit, but Christianity is so entwined with Western Civilization that it would seem a good idea to Read the full article...

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH’s rental housing is significantly older than its owner-occupied housing
item.description:
As America prepares to celebrate its 250th birthday, half of New Hampshire renters live in units built before the country celebrated its 200th birthday.  The median renter-occupied housing unit in New Hampshire was built in 1975, making it 51 years old this year, according to U.S. Census Bureau data.  By contrast, the median renter-occupied unit […]

The post NH’s rental housing is significantly older than its owner-occupied housing appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] No, Sorry, the War Doesn’t Mean “Renewables” Will Replace Oil
item.description:
Right on schedule, the climate activists and their corporate backers are capitalizing on wartime fuel shortages to claim that now, finally, we can get serious about fighting climate change. On March 15, The New York Times weighed in with an article titled “How War in Iran Could Remake the Global Energy Landscape.” Claiming the oil crisis could...

The post No, Sorry, the War Doesn’t Mean “Renewables” Will Replace Oil first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Federal Options for Large Scale Seawater Desalination
item.description:
The Carlsbad desalination plant is reportedly operating at half-capacity, basically because it’s less expensive to import water from the Colorado River. That is likely to change, as Arizona’s Department of Water Resources is negotiating with the San Diego County Water Authority to purchase some of the water they get from the Colorado River. The funds from Arizona will...

The post Federal Options for Large Scale Seawater Desalination first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] What’s Really in the Governor’s Final Supplemental Budget Package
item.description:
Supplemental budgets are often framed as routine housekeeping, minor adjustments to keep state finances on track. The Governor’s 2026 supplemental, however, was anything but routine. It represented a troubling fiscal expansion that risked driving up costs for Maine families and taxpayers. The Governor did not stop there, however, and has since released a Change Package […]

The post What’s Really in the Governor’s Final Supplemental Budget Package appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho’s housing problem may come down to one step too many
item.description: The two-stairway requirement acts as a binding constraint on supply, especially for small and mid-size apartment buildings. It doesn’t just make construction more expensive—it makes many projects impossible to build at all. Entire categories of housing that would otherwise be viable simply never get proposed.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri’s Film Tax Credits Still Don’t Add Up
item.description:
For some reason, film tax credits remain popular in Jefferson City. They are much less popular with economists. Missouri lawmakers are once again debating whether to extend the state’s film […]

The post Missouri’s Film Tax Credits Still Don’t Add Up appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1453 — Approp, H&W, rural health, add’l (-3)
item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. NOTE: This […]

item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. item.description:
Mayor Brandon Johnson’s ‘Cut the Tape’ initiative has made real gains for housing in its second year. Still, the hardest reforms remain undone.

item.description:
April 1, 2026 In politics, few ideas are as durable and as consistently disappointing as price controls. When...

item.description: Lawmakers can do more to protect access, affordability, and medical innovation

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Former teachers’ union president faces discipline for online celebration of Kirk slaying
item.description:
Former Martin County Education Association president and social studies teacher Matt Theobald appeared before the Florida Division of Administrative Hearings today as he fights to keep his job following a series of inflammatory social media posts made in the wake of Charlie Kirk’s assassination.

The post Former teachers’ union president faces discipline for online celebration of Kirk slaying appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Free Money Trap: How Laundered Compassion Fuels a Culture of Abuse and Fraud
item.description:
The headlines are, by now, a wearying routine. In 2025, a federal takedown revealed $14.6 billion in Medicare fraud, involving kickbacks for allegedly unnecessary tests. In Minnesota, the Feeding our Future scandal saw $300 million vanish from a pandemic meal program, while childcare fraud drained another $100 million via ghost students. California, meanwhile, became the...

The post The Free Money Trap: How Laundered Compassion Fuels a Culture of Abuse and Fraud first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: The Final Days - and What Could Come Next
item.description:
We are officially in the final stretch of the legislative session. Sine Die — the last day of the regular session — is set for April 5. That means lawmakers have just days left to finalize remaining legislation, negotiate the state budget, and bring this year’s work to a close. But as things stand right […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: The Final Days - and What Could Come Next appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] 17,000 Students, One Clear Message: Fully Fund LA GATOR
item.description:
Louisiana families have spoken—and they are demanding more educational opportunity. The latest application data for the LA GATOR program, released by the Louisiana Department of Education, shows overwhelming demand for school choice across the state. For the 2026–2027 school year, 17,848 students applied, with 16,993 deemed eligible for the program. These are not abstract numbers....

The post 17,000 Students, One Clear Message: Fully Fund LA GATOR appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] West Virginia Enacts Right to Try for Individualized Treatments
item.description:
In a life-affirming victory for rare disease patients, West Virginia became the 17th state to enact the Goldwater Institute’s Right..

The post West Virginia Enacts Right to Try for Individualized Treatments appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana’s Nursing Home Moratorium Is Protectionism
item.description:
When the government makes it illegal to open new nursing homes or add beds where people need care, that is not health policy. It is protectionism.  And in Louisiana, HB 199 would keep that protectionism in place for years longer by extending the state’s moratorium on new nursing facilities and additional beds. Supporters may call...

The post Louisiana’s Nursing Home Moratorium Is Protectionism appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] TEFA is a Success. What’s Next?
item.description:
Texas’ launch of the Education Freedom Accounts (EFA) program has been nothing short of historic. It sends a clear message that families are demanding more control over their children’s education. Within the first 24 hours, more than 42,000 applications poured in, shattering national records for a school choice program rollout. By the time applications closed,...

The post TEFA is a Success. What’s Next? first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation-Backed Bill Would Expand worker Freedom in Alabama
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today announced its support for House Bill 666 introduced by Alabama state Rep. Ernie Yarbrough (R-Decatur) that would end payroll dues deduction for school district and higher education employees and end opt-out window requirements for all Alabama public employees statewide.

The post Freedom Foundation-Backed Bill Would Expand worker Freedom in Alabama appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinois can expand opportunity with apprenticeship bill
item.description:
A measure in the General Assembly would permit cosmetology and barber apprenticeships as a pathway to a license.

The post Illinois can expand opportunity with apprenticeship bill appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Womb & Board
item.description:
Rising housing costs are delaying marriage and childbearing by preventing young adults from forming independent households, thereby making housing affordability a central driver of declining family formation and demographic stability. Key points: Housing affordability directly shapes whether young adults can form independent households, influencing marriage, fertility, and long-term family stability. Rising housing costs and limited...

The post Womb & Board first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] In memory of Tom Howatt, embodiment of American Dream
item.description:
Tom Howatt, who rose up as the son of a Scottish immigrant to become President and CEO of Wausau Paper, a preeminent leader of Wisconsin’s business community and an exceedingly generous philanthropist, has died at the age of 76 after a two-year battle with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). Howatt, who was also a long-time chairman [...]

The post In memory of Tom Howatt, embodiment of American Dream appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Forty-year-old vehicle emissions program under new scrutiny
item.description:
Momentum is growing to end vehicle emissions testing programs in several states, including Wisconsin.

The post Forty-year-old vehicle emissions program under new scrutiny appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Keeping the Taxpayer’s Voice at the Forefront
item.description:
More than 200 attendees filled ITR Foundation’s eighth annual Tax Day Luncheon, highlighting its role as a signature gathering for advancing taxpayer-focused policy and celebrating Iowa’s recent fiscal reforms under Governor Reynolds’ leadership. Governor Reynolds emphasized Iowa’s progress—from a 3.8% flat income tax to regulatory and government reforms—and underscored that the next major priority is property tax reform driven by controlling government spending. Reynolds praised ITR’s role in the legislative process, stating it was “the only one at the table representing the taxpayer,” reinforcing ITR’s unique position as a consistent voice for taxpayers at the Capitol.

The post Keeping the Taxpayer’s Voice at the Forefront appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Bus rapid transit was originally supposed to provide light-rail-like services...

The post TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.description:
As Washington threatens to crack down on fraud and abuse in New York’s Medicaid program, state legislators are doing their....

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Three questions about education spending in Idaho, and where we go from here
item.description: Let’s start with the basic reality: Idaho is increasing public education spending even as student enrollment levels off or declines.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] The Massachusetts Economy and the Inflation Reduction Act’s Medicine Price Controls
item.description:
Questions abound about the resilience of the Massachusetts economy.  Pioneer Institute has documented that high-net-worth individuals are fleeing the state for states with friendlier tax environments.  There is also a question about whether small businesses in the state can sustain and thrive in a business environment increasingly characterized by high...

The post The Massachusetts Economy and the Inflation Reduction Act’s Medicine Price Controls  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Students can’t be expected to learn in schools plagued by violence
item.description:
Students can’t be expected to learn in schools plagued by violence By Rafael Perez | April 3, 2026 A U.S. News & World Report comparison of public schools ranks California’s Pre-K-12 schools 38th in the nation. In California, 51.2% of our students fail to meet English standards and 62.7% fail to meet appropriate math standards. […]

The post Students can’t be expected to learn in schools plagued by violence appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] NYC Parks Department focuses resources on racism
item.description:
Chuck Ross writes for the Washington Free Beacon about questionable priorities for a New York City government agency. The New York City Parks Department, facing a $33 million budget cut and chronic understaffing problems, instructs its supervisors to be “antiracist” activists who police “microaggressions” and promote conversations about race in the workplace, documents obtained by...

The post NYC Parks Department focuses resources on racism appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Questioning Justice Jackson’s grasp of the law
item.description:
M.D. Kittle writes for the Federalist about the latest pronouncement from US Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson. During Tuesday’s oral arguments on a widely-watched birthright citizenship case, Jackson once again showed that a basic understanding of the law is no longer a prerequisite to serve on the nation’s highest court. No three words uttered...

The post Questioning Justice Jackson’s grasp of the law appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Placing global warming claims in perspective
item.description:
John Hinderaker writes for the Powerline blog about facts surrounding global temperatures. Hysteria about global warming stems from the fact that, as best one can estimate, the Earth’s average temperature has increased by around 1.1 degree C over the last century. Global temperatures have fluctuated widely in the past, and it has long been debated...

The post Placing global warming claims in perspective appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Courting Disaster: What Do New Social Media Rulings Mean for the Future of Free Speech and American Innovation?
item.description:
Last week, juries in New Mexico and California reached verdicts against Meta and YouTube of $375 million in civil penalties and $6 million for an individual plaintiff, respectively. These rulings are a harbinger of legal action to come, wherein states and individuals take to the courts in response to social problems they associate with social...

The post Courting Disaster: What Do New Social Media Rulings Mean for the Future of Free Speech and American Innovation? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.description:
With health insurance premiums climbing across New York, some lawmakers are once again pitching a familiar “solution” — scrap private coverage and put the state in charge of everyone’s health care. More than 30 members of the state Senate are beating the drum for the New York Health Act, which would force every resident into […]

item.description:

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Lawmakers Divided on Affordable Healthcare, Patients Aren’t
item.description:
Rising healthcare costs continue to squeeze household finances. Washington is divided over how to respond. But new public opinion data suggest that patients agree on an answer. More than eight in 10 voters say they would react positively to an elected official who believed that “[t]o improve health care, we need to fund patients, not […]

The post Lawmakers Divided on Affordable Healthcare, Patients Aren’t appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget
item.description:
Over the last several months, Maine Policy Institute has repeatedly pointed out the many problems that exist in Governor Mills’ 2026 supplemental budget. Maine lawmakers had an opportunity to fix serious flaws in the budget. Instead, the Legislature’s Appropriations Committee has taken an already problematic proposal from Janet Mills, doubled down, and made it worse. […]

The post From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Minnesota highest graduation rate paired with lowest proficiency
item.description:
The Minnesota Department of Education recently announced that the class of 2025 set a new record for high school graduation rates. At 84.9 percent, the four-year graduation rate is up from last year’s rate of 84.2 percent. “This is a moment worth celebrating,” said Gov. Tim Walz. “Decades in the classroom have helped inform many...

The post Minnesota highest graduation rate paired with lowest proficiency appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] New nuclear power, except in Minnesota
item.description:
From the Worchester (MA) Telegram & Gazette, Bipartisan New England governors form pact to grow nuclear energy. The Telegram reports, All New England governors issued a joint statement March 31 saying they would be exploring advanced nuclear energy technology. Healey, Lamont, Mills and McKee are Democrats, while Ayotte and Scott are Republicans. The governors are...

The post New nuclear power, except in Minnesota appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Just the Facts: Property Taxes in Texas’ Most Populous Cities, Counties, and School Districts 4th Edition
item.description:
Property taxes imposed by local governments have, in many instances, outpaced population growth and inflation. As a result, Texans pay some of the highest property taxes in the nation. Key points: Property taxes are assessed and collected by local governing bodies. The State does not levy a property tax. Local governments levied $89.4 billion in...

The post Just the Facts: Property Taxes in Texas’ Most Populous Cities, Counties, and School Districts 4th Edition first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.
